era_context: Early Bronze Age III; Old Kingdom Egypt; Early Dynastic III Mesopotamia. Civilisations in the Nile, Tigris–Euphrates, and Indus valleys are mature and expanding. Writing is established in Egypt and Mesopotamia. Most of the world is preliterate.
disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified. No specific events are attested for this year in any known source.
historiographic_note: 3062 BCE is not documented in any surviving contemporary source. The year falls within the Old Kingdom of Egypt and Early Dynastic III Mesopotamia, but no events, rulers, or significant occurrences are recorded for this specific year. The absence of documentation reflects both the limitations of the surviving record and the fact that year-by-year historical narrative is not possible for this period.
